title: offline signature verification
check: the attestation's proof (EcdsaSecp256k1Signature2019) verifies against the issuer DID's assertion key; DID resolution via the local Gatekeeper only, no other network

title: merkle commitment well-formed
check: each evidence group's commitment has alg 'sha256', a 64-hex merkleRoot, and artefactIds equal to 'merkle:sha256:'+merkleRoot

title: revealed-leaf merkle membership
check: every revealed leaf re-derives to the signed merkleRoot via its path (vacuously true in FULL/summary mode, where nothing is revealed)
source: e2e-evidence-selective: 'revealed leaf verifies against the signed Merkle root'

title: window coverage
check: the evidence group's [observedFrom, observedTo] covers the requested period at date granularity (observedFrom date <= from AND observedTo date >= to)

title: count threshold
check: the bundle establishes the predicate count >= 90 (the exact count is not required — a coarsened threshold assertion also satisfies this)

title: evidence kind matches
check: every evidence group's kind is the requested 'location'

title: observation window well-ordered
check: each evidence group has observedFrom <= observedTo

title: no out-of-window leaf (absence)
check: NO revealed leaf's observedAt falls outside the requested window — an absence claim; holds by construction in FULL mode

title: no third-party identifier (absence)
check: the only did: values in the bundle are the issuer, the credentialSubject, and the credentialSchema; no witness DID, pairwise linkage, or counterparty DID appears — an absence claim
source: CGPR §4.4(1)/(7) pairwise unlinkability; lexon_pvm absence lesson

title: no raw payload disclosed (absence)
check: the bundle carries only hash commitments, never artefact content: no ciphertext / payload / rawContent field appears anywhere — an absence claim
source: evidence-graph design: issuer-attested, provenance as content hashes

title: no linkage identifier (absence)
check: credentialSubject carries no cross-context linkage: no accountHandle, pairwise map, or foreign-id field beyond its did: subject — an absence claim

title: credential type
check: top-level type includes both 'VerifiableCredential' and 'HearthholdAttestation'

title: subject type
check: credentialSubject.type equals 'HearthholdAttestation'

title: context declared
check: @context includes the W3C VC 2.0 context and the Hearthold evidence context https://hearthold.dev/2026/evidence/v1

title: schema resolvable and conformant
check: credentialSchema.id resolves via the local Gatekeeper to a JsonSchema, and the bundle satisfies it (carries the required 'type')

title: machine-derived description
check: credentialSubject.descriptionSource equals 'machine-derived' (the Warden authors the disclosure; it is never requester-authored)
source: invariant: the Warden authors all consent text

title: declared trust class
check: credentialSubject.trustClass is one of 'witnessed' or 'composite'

title: evidence mirror consistent
check: the top-level evidence array is deep-equal to credentialSubject.evidence (the disclosed mirror matches the signed subject)

title: single-use txn consistent
check: credentialSubject.txn equals termsOfUse[0].txn (the burn identifier is internally consistent)
source: minted attestation: txn in subject + termsOfUse

title: issuer is an authorized asserter
check: the issuer DID resolves via the Gatekeeper and the key named by proof.verificationMethod is present in the issuer DID document's assertionMethod set
source: e2e-prove: 'proof from an untrusted issuer is rejected'

title: expiry declared and future
check: validUntil is present, ISO-parseable, and strictly greater than the check-time clock

title: single-use declared
check: termsOfUse carries an entry of type 'HearthholdSingleUse' with a non-empty txn

title: temporal sanity
check: validFrom is present, ISO-parseable, and <= validUntil
